rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cybernet Entertainment, Llc'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018 Line 1, DEFENDANT CYBERNET ENTERTAINMENT, LLC's Motion To Compel Plaintiff'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set Two) A key issue in this case is whether plaintiff's participation in the May 3, 2013 filming of a sex video was a cause of his HIV infection. Asserting that it is highly probable he was infected from April 20 to May 13, 2013 (based on his doctor's hearsay opinion), plaintiff provided a list of his sexual partners in that time frame and the partners' HIV test results. Defendant Cybernet Entertainment, LLC, however, seeks to further compel discovery of such information from four months before and six weeks after May 3, 2013. Cybernet says, inter alia, that plaintiff's sex partners in those time periods "may be potential sources of HIV exposure." Plaintiff contends that Cybernet's discovery would be irrelevant and invade privacy.Because the "window of exposure" for HIV infection is within the ken of medical experts, I gave both sides the opportunity to submit expert declarations. "Based on a reasonable degree of medical certainty," plaintiff's expert puts the "window" at April 15, 2013 to May 7, 2013. Cybertnet's expert first places the opening date of the window at April 10, 2013, but then says testing variance could put it as early as March 16. Cybernet's expert puts the end date at May 12. Thus, all three doctors agree on the end date within six days: May 13, May 7, May 12 - and all those dates are within the time frame for which plaintiff has already provided discovery. There is also significant agreement on the start date: April 20, April 15, April 10 (though Cybertnet's expert offers two dates). Most importantly, none of the doctors adopts anything approaching the 5.5-month period for which Cybernet's motion demands discovery. The motion is DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: JOHN DOE VS. KINK.COM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion To Compel The Person Most Qualified For Defendant Armory Studios, Llc'S Answers To Deposition Questions And All Related Follow-Up Questions At His Further Deposition And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018, Line 6, PLAINTIFF JOHN DOE Plaintiff'S Motion To Compel The Person Most Qualified For Defendant Armory Studios, Llc'S Answers To Deposition Questions And All Related Follow-Up Questions At His Further Deposition And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The issues raised in this motion are currently the subject of a writ review by the 1st District Court of Appeal in which the DCA has stayed enforcement of a prior order issued by Judge Kahn. In light of the stay and pending decision by the DCA, this motion is ordered off calendar. After the DCA issues a ruling on the writ, the parties are directed to meet and confer. If any issues remain outstanding, this motion should be re-noticed for hearing. If there is an exigent need for the information, an ex parte motion should be presented to Dept 302 to advance the hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

The Court finds that Defendant's response is code compliant. However, Defendant is directed to produce a privilege log regarding any documents withheld by October 26, 2018; (2) As to RFPD Nos, 1, 15, 29, 155-57, 161, 171-172, 175-76, 179-180, 183-84, 203-4, 212 - Defendant shall provide a supplemental response confirming that all responsive, non-privileged documents have been produced; (3) As to RFPD Nos, 80-85, 87, 94, 163-66, 191-194 - the Defendant shall provide a supplemental response identifying any person or entity who is in possession of the documents; (4) As to RFPD Nos. 30-36, 64, 211 - the Court finds that these requests implicate privacy rights - Defendant shall submit the files for an in camera review within 7 days after the DCA rules on the pending writ; (5) As to RFPD Nos 95, 107, 214-19 - granted; 222-23 denied. Sanctions denied. rulings.Case Title: FREZGI GEBREZGIABHER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018 Line 9, Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Summary Judgment Defendant City and County of San Francisco's ("CCSF") motion for summary judgment is GRANTED. There is no triable issue of material fact that a dangerous condition of public property caused plaintiff's injury as a matter of law. Plaintiff must demonstrate the following to maintain his cause of action for dangerous condition of public property: (1) that CCSF owned or maintained the property; (2) that the property was in a dangerous condition on the date of accident; (3) causation; (4) the dangerous condition created a reasonably foreseeable risk of the kind of injury incurred; and (5) either CCSF or its employee caused the condition or CCSF had actual or constructive notice of the dangerous condition a sufficient time prior to the time of the accident. (See Gov't Code §§ 830 & 835.) In this case, plaintiff cannot prove the existence of a dangerous condition of public property. Under Gov't Code § 830(a), a dangerous condition is "a condition or property that creates a substantial (as distinguished from a minor, trivial or insignificant) risk of injury when such property or adjacent property is used with due care in a manner in which it is reasonably foreseeable that it will be used." Gov't Code § 830.2 explains that: "A condition is not a dangerous condition within the meaning of this chapter if the trial or appellate court, viewing the evidence most favorably to the plaintiff, determines as a matter of law that the risk created by the condition was of such a minor, trivial or insignificant nature in view of the surrounding circumstances that no reasonable person would conclude that the condition created a substantial risk of injury when such property...was used with due care in a manner in which it was reasonably foreseeable that it would be used." The undisputed evidence shows that there was no dangerous condition of public property and either plaintiff's or Mr. Nguyen's negligence-or a combination of both-caused the accident as a matter of law. The dashcam video and still photographs that are attached to the declaration of Mr. Rosenblatt and authenticated by Mr. Nguyen demonstrate that no reasonable trier of fact could conclude otherwise. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for CCSF is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to discuss the following tentative: Granted. Defendants are correct that they cannot be forced to make contentions that they have yet to determine or assert. That being said, Defendants' responses that say "unknown" to the contention do not directly answer the interrogatory. If Defendants truly do not have any contentions on the issues raised in the interrogatories, the responses need to specifically say that. Supplemental responses to be served by October 29, 2018. Sanction requests by both parties to be addressed at hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA BERRY ET AL VS. SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Melissa Berry, Karizma Berry, Kharma Berry, Loyal Terrell Pryor'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,560.00 From Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018, Line 5, Plaintiffs Melissa Berry, Karizma Berry, Kharma Berry, Loyal Terrell Pryor'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Demands For Production Of Documents And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $4,560.00 From Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court's order of 10/5/18 is vacated. Hearing required to discuss the following: The Court agrees with Plaintiff that Defendants' responses are not code compliant, i.e. CCP 2031.210 requires that the response not simply list the documents being produced but that "all responsive, non privileged documents, will be produced." Defendants responses do not say that. That being said, some of the requests at issue seem overbroad and vague. It is unclear to the Court whether Defendant has withheld any documents based on its overbroad objections. The parties should be prepared to address the specific requests in dispute in which there is a question about the scope of the information being requested. Sanction requests by both parties to be addressed at hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MELISSA BERRY ET AL VS. SUTTER VALLEY HOSPITALS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,660.00 From Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF MELISSA BERRY, KARIZMA BERRY, KHARMA BERRY, LOYAL TERRELL PRYOR's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,660.00 From Defendant Sutter Valley Hospitals, Inc. Dba Sutter Tracy Community Hospital By Plaintiff Melissa Berry, By And Through Successors In Interest. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to discuss the following tentative: Motion granted in part; denied as follows: Form Interrogatory 12.1 - Granted. Defendants must answer the subparts to the interrogatory and not refer to documents. Form Interrogatory 17.1 - RFA #1 - denied. Answered and admitted. RFA #2 - denied. Answered and admitted. RFA # 3 - 6 - grant. The objections are overruled. If Defendants contend to be unable to admit or deny, they must fully answer the subparts to the interrogatory. RFA #8 - 10, 14- denied. Calls for a legal conclusion and expert opinion. RFA #12, 15, 16, 17, 20-24 -grant. Defendants' responses give a qualified answer. It is not allowed to rephrase the RFAs. RFA #18 -19 denied. Overbroad. Special Interrogatories, Set One #1 -2 - denied. Overbroad. # 3- 4, 10- 11, 15- 16, 18-19, 21- 22 - grant. Defendants' reference to documents is not proper. Information must be specifically provided. Supplemental responses to be served by October 29, 2018. Sanction requests by both parties to be addressed at hearing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018, Line 7, Defendant John Kaufman'S Motion For Admissions And Genuineness Of Documents Be Deemed Admitted. rulings.Case Title: ANDREY KUKUSHKIN VS. MED THRIVE COOPERATIVE, INC. DBA MEDITHRIVE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Arman Javids Notice Of Joinder And Joinder In Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative Inc Dba Medithrive Inc Misha Breyburg And Sasha Plotitsas Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, October 12, 2018 Line 8, DEFENDANT ARMAN JAVID's Joinder In Defendants Med Thrive Cooperative Inc Dba Medithrive Inc Misha Breyburg And Sasha Plotitsas Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint Before filing any demurrer, counsel "shall meet and confer in person or by telephone." (CCP §430.41(a) (emphasis added). Counsel's declaration indicates that did not occur here, so the demurrer and its associated motion to strike are ordered OFF CALENDAR. Moreover and in any event, leave to amend is liberally permitted by California's courts, so I GRANT plaintiff's request to file a second amended complaint. (See CCP §§473(a), 576; Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 490.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu
